location.office_time_required: Flex First — employees choose where they do their best work; regular in-person events

level_signals: 7+ years of work experience post-graduation in a technology field
Driving architectural and strategic decisions at org leadership level
Base pay range up to $216,500 in top-tier states, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of work experience post-graduation in a technology field, preferably as a technical program or project manager, product manager, software engineer (technical domain), or related technical discipline.

Demonstrated ability to create functional specs, set program structure and influence program strategy. Previous experience in managing complex multi-org programs end-to-end, from definition to launch, throughout a software development lifecycle.
A strong communicator with the ability to distill multiple streams of information, convey priorities and risks, and build a narrative for stakeholders.
A strong sense of ownership and intellectual curiosity to understand executional and technical problems in depth, identify potential issues and offer actions to prevent them.
Excellent collaborator and motivator, able to bring a team together to align and deliver on a common goal. Can build trust and partnerships with senior Engineering and Product leaders.
Growth mindset - seeks and is receptive to actionable feedback and growth opportunities.
Takes accountability seriously; can operate independently to deliver responsibilities and milestones on time and within budget.
Self-motivated and thrives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor's degree in a technology / engineering discipline.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in a technology/engineering discipline.
Direct experience working on Ads Technology and experiences is a big plus.

Demonstrated ability to showcase deep technical domain knowledge on the programs that you take on, enabling you to be seen as a strong technical voice in the program and adding significant value towards architecting a program.
Demonstrated ability to drive architectural and strategic decisions at the org leadership level.
Extensive previous experience around creating innovative program management processes/tools/approaches to deliver company outcomes reliably and efficiently.

scope.surface: Ads & Data Organization — ads technology programs across Instacart's multi-sided marketplace
primary_metrics_inferred: Program delivery on time and within budget
Quality of execution across multi-org programs
Alignment and adoption of org-wide program management processes
core_responsibilities: Lead planning and high quality technical execution of large Ads domain engineering programs
Work with cross-functional partners to set project objectives and metric-based success criteria
Drive definition of project requirements and execution plans
Own clear and effective communication with stakeholders on priorities, risks, and changes
Foster a culture of teamwork and shared goals within the program team
Contribute to Instacart's success by suggesting and driving organization-wide initiatives

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across multiple US geographies
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Flex First remote-first policy — role is fully remote-eligible
Clear scope: Ads & Data Organization with specific domain focus
JD uses the #LI-Remote tag, confirming remote eligibility
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Compensation range is wide across states ($178K–$216.5K), creating pay equity ambiguity for remote workers
7+ years minimum is a hard floor with no stated equivalent-experience pathway
Preferred qualifications around 'deep technical domain knowledge' and 'architectural decisions' may function as de facto requirements in practice

fields_inferred_not_stated: compensation.base_range_usd lower bound (taken from lowest state tier: All other states $178K)
ambiguities: JD body includes #LI-Remote and describes Flex First policy allowing full remote, but also references 'regular in-person events' — treated as remote-us with optional in-person community events, not a formal hybrid requirement.

Preferred qualification around 'deep technical domain knowledge' and 'architectural decisions' is listed under Preferred but may carry significant weight for a Senior TPM role; classified as soft/nice-to-have per JD structure.
